meta.name: Ghanaian Groundnut Soup
meta.subtitle: Rich peanut stew/soup with aromatics, tomatoes, and optional meats

source.name: ssa
notes: Canonical Ghana-style groundnut soup built on peanut paste and tomato-onion base.
Designed for SSA planners: batch-friendly, freezer-friendly, adaptable for different proteins and heat levels.

yield.notes: Serves 6 as a main with fufu/rice balls/omotuo, or 8 as a starter.

optional.label: Dutch oven / heavy pot
optional.notes: Helps prevent scorching and keeps a steady simmer.

ingredients.label: Natural peanut butter (unsweetened) or ground roasted peanuts paste
notes: Use unsweetened peanut butter (just peanuts + salt).
If using peanut paste (ground roasted peanuts), use the same volume.

ingredients.label: Tomatoes (Roma or plum), chopped
ingredients.notes: Or substitute 1 can (28 oz) crushed tomatoes.

ingredients.label: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Optional but common for a sweeter, fuller tomato-pepper base.

ingredients.label: Hot pepper (scotch bonnet or habanero)
ingredients.notes: Use whole for gentle heat, or blend a piece in for more heat.

ingredients.label: Protein (optional): goat, beef, or chicken pieces
notes: Traditional versions often use goat or beef; chicken is common too.
If using meat, brown/simmer until tender before adding peanut mix.

ingredients.label: Stock (chicken/beef) or water
ingredients.notes: Start with 8 cups; you can thin later to your preferred soup consistency.

ingredients.label: Cayenne or chili flakes
ingredients.notes: Optional if you didn’t use a hot pepper.

ingredients.label: Smoked dried fish (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional traditional umami booster. Remove bones and rinse if very salty.

ingredients.label: Greens (optional): spinach or kontomire/cocoyam leaves
ingredients.notes: Not always included; add near the end if desired.

steps.title: Blend aromatics and tomato base
instructions: In a blender, combine onion, tomatoes, garlic, ginger, and bell pepper (if using).
Blend until smooth (add a splash of water only if needed to move the blender).
Optional: add the hot pepper to the blender for more heat, or keep it whole for a gentler infusion.
checks: Base is smooth with no large chunks.

steps.title: Cook down the tomato base
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy pot over medium heat.
Add tomato paste and fry for 1–2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the raw smell fades.
Pour in the blended base carefully (it may sputter).
Add thyme, bay leaves (if using), black pepper, and the whole hot pepper (if keeping whole).
Simmer uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the base reduces and darkens slightly and the oil begins to show on top.
checks: Base thickens and deepens in color.
Oil begins to separate slightly (good sign).

steps.title: Add and simmer protein (optional)
instructions: If using meat/chicken: add it now and stir to coat in the base.
Add about 4 cups of stock/water (enough to partially cover).
Simmer until the protein is mostly tender.
If using smoked dried fish, add it during the last 10–15 minutes of this simmer.
checks: Protein is mostly tender before adding peanut mixture (to avoid over-thickening too early).

steps.title: Loosen peanut butter into a smooth slurry
instructions: In a bowl, whisk peanut butter with 2 cups of warm stock/water until completely smooth and pourable.
This prevents clumping and helps the soup thicken evenly.
checks: Peanut mixture is smooth with no lumps.

steps.title: Add peanut slurry and simmer gently
instructions: Reduce heat to low-medium so the pot is at a gentle simmer.
Slowly stir in the peanut slurry.
Add remaining stock/water to reach your preferred soup thickness (start by adding 2 more cups, then adjust).
Simmer gently, stirring frequently (peanut soups can scorch), until flavors meld and the soup thickens.
checks: Soup is gently bubbling (not boiling hard).
No scorching on bottom; stir well every few minutes.

steps.title: Season and finish (optional greens)
instructions: Taste and add salt gradually (stock saltiness varies). Add cayenne/chili flakes if you want more heat.
If adding greens, stir them in and simmer 2–3 minutes until just wilted.
Remove bay leaves and the whole hot pepper (if used whole).
checks: Salt/heat are balanced and peanut flavor is prominent.
Greens are wilted but still vibrant (if used).

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve hot with fufu, rice balls (omotuo), banku, kenkey, or plain rice.
Optional: garnish with extra chili oil or a squeeze of lime for brightness.

fridge.notes: Cool quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours. Peanut soups thicken as they chill—thin with water/stock when reheating.

freezer.notes: Freeze in portions. Thaw overnight and reheat gently, stirring often.

reheat.notes: Reheat on low, stirring frequently to prevent scorching; add water/stock to loosen.

batch.notes: Excellent make-ahead stew for busy weeks; freezes very well.

remixNotes: Thin leftovers slightly and serve over rice for a faster meal.
Add extra greens on reheat to refresh the pot.

notes: Keep the simmer gentle after adding peanut butter to reduce risk of splitting and scorching.
If the soup tastes flat, add a pinch more salt and simmer 5 minutes; peanut-based stews often need enough salt to 'open up.'
For a deeper smoky profile, add a small piece of smoked fish or a pinch of smoked paprika.

adapt.notes: Use vegetable stock, omit meats/fish, and add extra greens + mushrooms for body.
Simmer goat until tender before peanut slurry; goat benefits from a longer pre-simmer (45–75 minutes depending on cut).
